sourceMovie.title: Atomic Blonde
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Atomic Blonde for the same reason it broke through in 2017: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a thriller rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. David Leitch keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and thriller blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Spectre
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Atomic Blonde clicked for you, Spectre (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in action and thriller, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Atomic Blonde?
faq.answer: Try Spectre, The Bourne Supremacy, Mission: Impossible III—each shares DNA with Atomic Blonde while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Atomic Blonde?
faq.answer: Atomic Blonde (2017) is best described as Action, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Atomic Blonde worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this action lane.
